summary: This notebook serves as a practical tutorial on fine-tuning the Gemma2-2B instruction model for Korean customer service email responses using LoRA and KerasNLP. It demonstrates the complete workflow from installing dependencies and configuring the JAX backend to loading a small dataset, applying strict prompt templates with turn markers, and filtering samples by token length. The training setup details LoRA rank configuration, AdamW optimization with weight decay, and a custom callback to save adapter weights after each epoch. Readers gain actionable insights into parameter-efficient fine-tuning, prompt engineering for instruction models, and how decoding strategies influence generation quality.
competition_flow: Loads a Korean cake-ordering dataset, formats inputs with instruction templates, fine-tunes Gemma2-2B using LoRA and KerasNLP, and demonstrates inference with varying prompts and decoding strategies.
data_reading: Uses datasets.load_dataset to fetch the "bebechien/korean_cake_boss" training split, converts it to numpy format, and iterates through rows manually.
data_processing: Applies a fixed prompt template with <start_of_turn> and <end_of_turn> markers, tokenizes inputs using the Gemma tokenizer, filters out samples exceeding 256 tokens, and caps the training set at 100 examples.
insights: LoRA enables efficient fine-tuning of large models by isolating trainable parameters to a low-rank adapter.
Instruction-tuned models require strict prompt formatting with turn markers to function correctly.
Token length filtering is essential for controlling memory usage and preventing training crashes.
Swapping decoding samplers post-training allows quick experimentation with output randomness without retraining.
